title: [EPIC] EP15: Design System & Component Library
body:
## Epic: Design System & Component Library

**Objective:** Build a reusable, accessible, and themeable design system that ensures visual consistency across the entire platform and accelerates frontend development.

**Phase:** 1 (Month 1-2)

**Key Outcomes:**
- Shared component library in React + Tailwind CSS
- Design tokens for colors, typography, spacing, shadows
- Dark mode support across all components
- Accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA) baked into every component
- Storybook documentation for all components
- Responsive breakpoints for desktop, tablet, and mobile

**Tech:** React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI primitives, Storybook

**Dependencies:** None (foundational)

**Related Stories:** #49 through #52

title: [STORY] Design token system and Tailwind theme configuration
body:
## User Story

**As a** frontend developer,
**I want** a centralized design token system with Tailwind theme configuration,
**So that** every component uses consistent colors, typography, spacing, and can be themed per-tenant (white-label for agency tier).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Design tokens defined: color palette (primary, secondary, semantic), typography scale (6 sizes), spacing scale (8px grid), border radius, shadows, z-index layers
- [ ] Tailwind config extends default theme with custom tokens
- [ ] CSS custom properties for runtime theming (dark mode, tenant branding)
- [ ] Dark mode support via `prefers-color-scheme` and manual toggle
- [ ] Color palette includes semantic colors: success (green), warning (amber), danger (red), info (blue)
- [ ] Agent-specific accent colors: listing (blue), inventory (green), advertising (orange), pricing (purple), analytics (teal), compliance (gray)
- [ ] Typography: Inter or similar variable font, weights 400/500/600/700
- [ ] Responsive breakpoints: mobile (< 640px), tablet (640-1024px), desktop (> 1024px), wide (> 1440px)

## Technical Notes
- Use CSS custom properties for values that change at runtime
- Tailwind `darkMode: 'class'` for manual dark mode toggle
- Export tokens as JSON for potential React Native / mobile reuse

## Epic: EP15 | Priority: P0 | Points: 5

title: [STORY] Core UI component library (atoms and molecules)
body:
## User Story

**As a** frontend developer,
**I want** a library of reusable, accessible UI components,
**So that** I can build dashboards and pages quickly without reinventing common patterns.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Atoms (primitive components)
- [ ] Button: primary, secondary, outline, ghost, danger, loading state, icon-only variants, sizes (sm/md/lg)
- [ ] Input: text, number, search, password with validation states (error, success, disabled)
- [ ] Select / Dropdown: single-select, multi-select, searchable, async loading
- [ ] Checkbox, Radio, Toggle Switch
- [ ] Badge / Tag: status badges (active, paused, error, pending), color variants, dismissible
- [ ] Avatar: image, initials fallback, status indicator dot
- [ ] Tooltip, Popover
- [ ] Spinner / Skeleton loader
- [ ] Progress bar (determinate and indeterminate)

### Molecules (composite components)
- [ ] Form field: label + input + helper text + error message
- [ ] Search bar with debounced input and suggestions
- [ ] Stat card: metric value + label + trend indicator (up/down arrow + percentage)
- [ ] Alert / Toast notification: info, success, warning, error with dismiss and action buttons
- [ ] Modal / Dialog: sizes (sm/md/lg/full), confirmation variant, form variant
- [ ] Dropdown menu with keyboard navigation
- [ ] Tabs component with lazy-loaded tab panels
- [ ] Breadcrumb navigation
- [ ] Pagination component
- [ ] Empty state: illustration + message + CTA button
- [ ] Data table: sortable, filterable, selectable rows, expandable rows, pagination

### Requirements
- [ ] All components support `ref` forwarding
- [ ] Keyboard navigation and ARIA attributes (WCAG 2.1 AA)
- [ ] Storybook stories for every component with interactive controls
- [ ] Unit tests for interaction states

## Technical Notes
- Build on Radix UI primitives for accessibility
- Use `cva` (class-variance-authority) for variant management
- Compound component pattern for complex components (Table, Tabs)

## Epic: EP15 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Chart and data visualization component library
body:
## User Story

**As a** frontend developer,
**I want** reusable chart components that match the design system,
**So that** I can build analytics dashboards with consistent, interactive visualizations.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Line chart: multi-series, time-series, hover tooltips, zoom/pan, responsive
- [ ] Bar chart: vertical, horizontal, stacked, grouped
- [ ] Area chart: stacked, gradient fill
- [ ] Pie / Donut chart: with center metric display
- [ ] Sparkline: mini inline chart for stat cards and table cells
- [ ] Funnel chart: for conversion funnel visualization
- [ ] Heatmap: for dayparting and time-based data
- [ ] KPI gauge: circular progress for target metrics (ACoS, ROAS)
- [ ] All charts respect design tokens (colors, fonts, dark mode)
- [ ] Responsive container that adapts to parent width
- [ ] Loading skeleton state for async data
- [ ] Export to PNG/SVG option
- [ ] Accessible: keyboard navigable data points, screen reader descriptions

## Technical Notes
- Use Recharts (React + D3) as base charting library
- Wrap in custom components with design system integration
- Memoize chart data to prevent re-render jank

## Epic: EP15 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Agent status and activity visualization components
body:
## User Story

**As a** frontend developer,
**I want** specialized components for displaying AI agent status, activity, and decision flows,
**So that** sellers can understand what agents are doing and why.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Agent status indicator: icon + name + status (active/idle/error/awaiting approval) with pulse animation for active
- [ ] Activity feed item: timestamp + agent icon + action description + status badge + expand for details
- [ ] Decision card: agent recommendation with reasoning summary, approve/reject buttons, confidence score
- [ ] Approval queue item: priority indicator + action summary + affected ASINs + impact estimate + approve/reject/batch-approve
- [ ] Agent orchestration flow: visual showing how agents coordinated (e.g., inventory → ads → pricing)
- [ ] Timeline component: chronological display of agent actions on an ASIN or campaign
- [ ] Diff viewer: before/after comparison for listing changes, price changes, bid changes
- [ ] Confidence meter: visual indicator of agent's confidence in a recommendation
- [ ] Notification bell with unread count and dropdown list

## Technical Notes
- Activity feed uses virtual scrolling for performance (thousands of items)
- Diff viewer uses react-diff-viewer or custom implementation
- Animate status transitions with CSS transitions, not JS animation libraries

## Epic: EP15 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP16: Frontend — Onboarding & Authentication Screens
body:
## Epic: Onboarding & Authentication Screens

**Objective:** Design and implement the complete onboarding journey from signup to first agent activation.

**Phase:** 1 (Month 2-3)

**Key Screens:**
- Landing/Marketing page
- Sign up / Sign in (Cognito)
- Amazon SP-API OAuth connection wizard
- Amazon Ads API connection wizard
- Plan selection & billing setup
- Agent configuration walkthrough
- Dashboard first-run experience

**Related Stories:** #54 through #56

title: [STORY] Onboarding wizard — Amazon account connection flow
body:
## User Story

**As a** new seller signing up,
**I want** a guided step-by-step wizard that connects my Amazon accounts and configures agents,
**So that** I can go from signup to automation in under 10 minutes without confusion.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Screen: Welcome & Account Creation
- [ ] Email/password signup or Google/Apple SSO via Cognito
- [ ] Company name and primary marketplace selection
- [ ] Clean, minimal form with progress indicator (step 1 of 5)

### Screen: Connect Amazon Seller Central
- [ ] Clear explanation of what permissions are needed and why
- [ ] "Connect Seller Central" button → LWA OAuth popup
- [ ] Real-time validation: green checkmarks as connection succeeds
- [ ] Error handling with plain-language troubleshooting steps
- [ ] Support for multiple marketplace connections (NA, EU, FE)

### Screen: Connect Amazon Advertising
- [ ] Separate OAuth for Ads API credentials
- [ ] Advertiser profile selection (if multiple brands)
- [ ] MCP Server connection test with status indicator
- [ ] Skip option (can connect later)

### Screen: Choose Plan & Billing
- [ ] Plan comparison cards: Starter, Growth, Professional, Enterprise
- [ ] Feature matrix highlighting differences
- [ ] Stripe checkout integration for payment
- [ ] Free trial option if applicable

### Screen: Configure Your Agents
- [ ] Business mode selector (growth vs profit) with visual explanation
- [ ] Per-agent autonomy sliders: full auto → approval required → disabled
- [ ] Key thresholds: min margin %, max ad spend/day, target ACoS
- [ ] Brand voice selection for listing content (professional, casual, technical)
- [ ] "Recommended" defaults based on business size

### Screen: Dashboard First-Run
- [ ] Guided tour highlighting key dashboard areas (Intercom-style)
- [ ] First agent action: "Let's optimize your top listing" walkthrough
- [ ] Checklist widget showing setup completion progress

## Design Requirements
- [ ] Illustrations at each step (custom or Lottie animations)
- [ ] Mobile responsive (sellers may sign up from phone)
- [ ] Progress bar showing completion percentage
- [ ] "Back" navigation without losing entered data
- [ ] Skip and return later capability (save partial onboarding state)

## Epic: EP16 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Authentication screens — sign in, sign up, password reset
body:
## User Story

**As a** returning seller,
**I want** fast, secure authentication with SSO options,
**So that** I can access my dashboard quickly without friction.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Sign in: email/password + "Remember me" + SSO buttons (Google, Apple)
- [ ] Sign up: name, email, password with strength indicator, terms acceptance
- [ ] Password reset: email verification flow with branded email template
- [ ] MFA setup screen (TOTP or SMS) — required for Enterprise tier
- [ ] Team member invitation accept screen
- [ ] Session timeout warning modal with "Keep me signed in" option
- [ ] Branded login page matching platform design system
- [ ] Error states: invalid credentials, account locked, expired session
- [ ] Cognito hosted UI customization or custom auth pages

## Epic: EP16 | Priority: P0 | Points: 5

title: [STORY] Plan selection and billing management screens
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** clear plan comparison and self-service billing management,
**So that** I can choose the right plan and manage my subscription without contacting support.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Plan comparison page: feature matrix, pricing, recommended badge, toggle monthly/annual
- [ ] Checkout flow: Stripe Elements embedded payment form
- [ ] Billing dashboard: current plan, next billing date, payment method, usage meters
- [ ] Usage breakdown: agent actions used vs included, ad spend managed vs tier limit
- [ ] Invoice history with download links
- [ ] Upgrade/downgrade flow with prorated billing explanation
- [ ] Cancel subscription flow with retention offer and feedback survey
- [ ] Add-on purchases: Marketplace Intelligence, extra agent credits
- [ ] Payment method update screen

## Epic: EP16 | Priority: P1 | Points: 5

title: [EPIC] EP17: Frontend — Main Dashboard & Navigation
body:
## Epic: Main Dashboard & Navigation

**Objective:** Design the primary dashboard experience — the home screen sellers see daily with navigation to all platform areas.

**Phase:** 1 (Month 3-4)

**Key Screens:**
- Main dashboard (command center)
- Sidebar navigation
- Global search
- Notification center
- Marketplace switcher
- Account settings

**Design Philosophy:** Dashboard should answer "What do I need to know right now?" and "What needs my attention?" within 3 seconds of loading.

title: [STORY] Main dashboard — command center layout
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a single dashboard that shows my business health, agent activity, and pending approvals at a glance,
**So that** I can make decisions and take action in under 30 seconds.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Layout Structure
- [ ] Collapsible sidebar navigation (icon-only on collapse)
- [ ] Top bar: marketplace selector, search, notifications bell, profile menu
- [ ] Main content area with configurable widget grid

### Dashboard Widgets (default layout)
- [ ] **Revenue Snapshot**: today's revenue, orders, units sold with sparkline vs yesterday/last week
- [ ] **Profit Summary**: today's estimated profit, margin %, TACoS, trend arrows
- [ ] **Agent Status Bar**: row of 6 agent icons with status dots (green=active, yellow=awaiting, red=error, gray=disabled)
- [ ] **Approval Queue** (prominent): count badge + list of top 5 pending actions with quick approve/reject
- [ ] **Inventory Alerts**: ASINs with < 14 days stock, stranded inventory count
- [ ] **Advertising Performance**: spend today, ACoS, ROAS, top/bottom 3 campaigns by performance
- [ ] **Listing Health**: total active/inactive/suppressed listings, recent quality score changes
- [ ] **Activity Feed**: real-time scrolling feed of agent actions with filters
- [ ] **Buy Box Tracker**: win rate trend, ASINs lost/gained today

### Interactions
- [ ] Click any widget to drill into the relevant module
- [ ] Drag-and-drop widget reordering (saved per user)
- [ ] Time range selector: today, 7d, 30d, custom
- [ ] Auto-refresh with configurable interval (30s / 1m / 5m)
- [ ] WebSocket-powered real-time updates for activity feed and metrics

### Responsive
- [ ] Desktop: 3-column grid
- [ ] Tablet: 2-column grid, sidebar collapsed
- [ ] Mobile: single column, bottom navigation bar

## Epic: EP17 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Sidebar navigation and global search
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** intuitive navigation that lets me find any feature or ASIN quickly,
**So that** I can efficiently manage my business without getting lost in the interface.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Sidebar Navigation
- [ ] Sections: Dashboard, Listings, Inventory, Advertising, Pricing, Analytics, Competitors, Compliance, Promotions, Orders, Settings
- [ ] Each section has an icon + label (label hidden when collapsed)
- [ ] Expandable sub-navigation for sections with multiple pages
- [ ] Active state highlighting with accent color
- [ ] Badge counts on nav items: pending approvals, inventory alerts, listing issues
- [ ] Collapse/expand toggle with smooth animation
- [ ] Pinned/favorites section for frequently used pages

### Global Search (Cmd+K / Ctrl+K)
- [ ] Spotlight-style search overlay
- [ ] Search across: ASINs, SKUs, campaign names, keywords, order IDs
- [ ] Recent searches and quick actions ("Create listing", "Check inventory", "New campaign")
- [ ] Keyboard navigation through results
- [ ] Search results grouped by category with icons

### Top Bar
- [ ] Marketplace selector dropdown (US, CA, UK, DE, etc.) with flag icons
- [ ] Notification bell with unread count and dropdown
- [ ] Profile menu: account settings, team, billing, help, sign out
- [ ] "Agent mode" toggle: show/hide agent activity overlay

## Epic: EP17 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Notification center and real-time alert system
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** real-time notifications about important events and agent actions,
**So that** I never miss critical alerts like stock-outs, listing suppressions, or budget overspend.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] In-app notification dropdown from bell icon in top bar
- [ ] Notification types: approval requests, alerts (stock, price, compliance), agent completions, system messages
- [ ] Priority levels: critical (red), warning (amber), info (blue), success (green)
- [ ] Mark as read, mark all as read, dismiss
- [ ] Full notification center page with filters and search
- [ ] Push notifications via browser (ServiceWorker) with permission prompt
- [ ] Email notification digests (configurable: instant, hourly, daily)
- [ ] WebSocket delivery for instant in-app updates
- [ ] Notification preferences page: per-type toggle for in-app, email, push
- [ ] Sound toggle for critical alerts

## Epic: EP17 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP18: Frontend — Listing Management Module
body:
## Epic: Listing Management Module

**Objective:** Design and build the listing management screens where sellers view, edit, and manage AI-optimized product listings.

**Phase:** 1 (Month 3-4)

**Key Screens:**
- Listing catalog view (table + grid)
- Listing detail / editor
- AI generation interface
- Listing quality dashboard
- Bulk operations view

title: [STORY] Listing catalog view with quality indicators
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a catalog view of all my listings with quality scores and status indicators,
**So that** I can quickly see which listings need attention and prioritize optimization.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Catalog Table View
- [ ] Columns: image thumbnail, ASIN/SKU, title (truncated), status (active/inactive/suppressed), quality score (color-coded), Buy Box %, price, FBA/FBM, BSR, last optimized date
- [ ] Sortable by any column
- [ ] Filterable: status, quality score range, category, fulfillment type, marketplace
- [ ] Search by ASIN, SKU, or title keyword
- [ ] Bulk selection with batch actions (optimize, update price, suppress)
- [ ] Pagination with configurable page size (25/50/100)
- [ ] Expandable row showing bullet points preview and agent recommendations

### Quality Score Visual
- [ ] Score 0-100 with color: red (0-40), amber (41-70), green (71-100)
- [ ] Breakdown tooltip: title score, bullets score, images score, backend keywords score
- [ ] Improvement opportunity badge ("AI can improve this")

### Quick Actions
- [ ] "Optimize with AI" button per listing → opens AI generation modal
- [ ] "View on Amazon" external link
- [ ] "Edit" → opens listing editor
- [ ] "History" → shows change timeline

### Grid View Toggle
- [ ] Card-based grid with product image, title, quality score badge, price, status
- [ ] Useful for visual catalog browsing

## Epic: EP18 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] AI listing generation and editor interface
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** an intuitive interface to generate AI-optimized listings and review/edit them before publishing,
**So that** I can leverage AI for speed while maintaining control over my brand voice.

## Acceptance Criteria

### AI Generation Panel (left side)
- [ ] Input fields: product name, key features, target keywords, product images, category
- [ ] "Generate" button with loading animation showing agent working
- [ ] Generation options: tone (professional/casual/technical), keyword density (light/moderate/aggressive)
- [ ] "Regenerate" with feedback: "Make it shorter", "Add more keywords", "More persuasive"
- [ ] Competitor reference: optionally paste competitor ASIN for competitive analysis

### Preview Panel (right side, live preview)
- [ ] Amazon product page mock-up showing title, bullets, description as they would appear
- [ ] Inline editing: click any text block to edit directly in preview
- [ ] Character count indicators with Amazon limit warnings (title ≤ 200 chars, etc.)
- [ ] Keyword highlighting: target keywords highlighted in generated content
- [ ] SEO score recalculation on each edit

### Diff View
- [ ] Side-by-side comparison: current live listing vs AI-proposed changes
- [ ] Additions highlighted green, removals highlighted red
- [ ] Per-section toggle: accept/reject title, bullets, description, search terms independently

### Actions
- [ ] "Approve & Publish" → sends to Amazon via Listings Items API
- [ ] "Save Draft" → stores for later review
- [ ] "Schedule" → publish at specific date/time
- [ ] Publishing status indicator: submitting → processing → live (or error with details)

## Epic: EP18 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [EPIC] EP19: Frontend — Advertising Dashboard
body:
## Epic: Advertising Dashboard

**Objective:** Design the PPC advertising management interface where sellers monitor campaigns, review AI optimizations, and control ad strategy.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 5-6)

**Key Screens:**
- Campaign overview dashboard
- Campaign detail view
- Keyword performance table
- Search term report
- Bid optimization history
- Ad spend vs revenue chart
- MCP interaction log

title: [STORY] Campaign overview dashboard with AI insights
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** an advertising dashboard that shows campaign performance with AI-generated insights and optimization suggestions,
**So that** I can understand my ad performance and approve AI-recommended optimizations from one screen.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Top Metrics Bar
- [ ] Total spend (today / period), total ad sales, ACoS, ROAS, TACoS, impressions, clicks, CTR, CPC, orders
- [ ] Each metric with trend arrow and % change vs previous period
- [ ] Clickable metrics for drill-down

### Campaign Table
- [ ] Columns: campaign name, type (SP/SB/SD), status (active/paused/archived), budget, spend, sales, ACoS, ROAS, impressions, clicks, orders
- [ ] Sortable, filterable by type/status/performance range
- [ ] Inline actions: pause/resume, edit budget, view details
- [ ] Color-coded ACoS cells (green = below target, amber = at target, red = above target)
- [ ] "AI Recommendation" column: icons showing pending optimizations per campaign
- [ ] Expand row to see ad groups and top keywords

### AI Insights Panel
- [ ] Top 5 AI recommendations with estimated impact: "Increase bid on 'wireless earbuds' — est. +15% impressions"
- [ ] One-click approve or reject per recommendation
- [ ] "Approve All Safe" batch button for low-risk optimizations
- [ ] Search term harvest suggestions: new keywords found in Auto campaigns
- [ ] Negative keyword suggestions: wasteful search terms to exclude

### Charts
- [ ] Spend vs Sales over time (dual-axis line chart)
- [ ] ACoS trend line with target threshold line
- [ ] Top 10 keywords by spend (horizontal bar chart)
- [ ] Dayparting heatmap: performance by hour × day-of-week

### MCP Activity Log
- [ ] Expandable section showing recent MCP Server interactions
- [ ] Each entry: timestamp, prompt sent, action taken, result status
- [ ] Useful for debugging and understanding AI ad decisions

## Epic: EP19 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Keyword and search term management interface
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a dedicated interface to manage keywords and search terms across campaigns,
**So that** I can see all targeting data in one place and approve AI keyword recommendations.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Master keyword table: keyword, match type, campaigns using it, total spend, total sales, ACoS, impressions, clicks, conversions
- [ ] Search term report: customer search terms, matched keyword, spend, sales, conversion rate
- [ ] AI keyword suggestions: new keywords to add with estimated search volume and competition
- [ ] Negative keyword manager: current negatives + AI suggestions for new negatives
- [ ] Keyword gap analysis: competitor keywords seller is not targeting
- [ ] Bulk keyword actions: add to campaigns, change match type, adjust bids, add as negative
- [ ] Keyword performance trends over time
- [ ] Filter by campaign, ad group, performance tier

## Epic: EP19 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP20: Frontend — Inventory Management Module
body:
## Epic: Inventory Management Module

**Objective:** Design the inventory management screens for monitoring stock, viewing forecasts, and managing replenishment.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 5-6)

**Key Screens:**
- Inventory overview dashboard
- Product-level inventory detail
- Demand forecast visualization
- Replenishment planning interface
- Inbound shipment tracker

title: [STORY] Inventory dashboard with forecast visualization
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** an inventory dashboard that shows current stock levels, projected stock-out dates, and AI-generated replenishment recommendations,
**So that** I never stock out or over-order.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Inventory Overview
- [ ] Summary cards: total SKUs, healthy stock (>30 days), low stock (7-30 days), critical (<7 days), out of stock, excess (>90 days)
- [ ] Inventory health donut chart with click-through to filtered lists
- [ ] Total inventory value and estimated days of stock

### Inventory Table
- [ ] Columns: image, ASIN/SKU, product name, FBA qty, FBM qty, inbound qty, reserved, days of stock, daily velocity, reorder point, status badge
- [ ] Color-coded status: green (healthy), amber (reorder soon), red (critical/OOS)
- [ ] Sort by urgency (critical first), stock level, velocity
- [ ] Quick action: "Create Replenishment Order" button

### Demand Forecast Chart (per ASIN detail view)
- [ ] Historical sales line + AI forecast line with confidence interval shading
- [ ] Projected stock-out date marked with vertical red line
- [ ] Reorder point line overlaid
- [ ] Seasonal events annotated (Prime Day, Black Friday, etc.)
- [ ] Toggle: 30/60/90-day forecast horizon

### Replenishment Planner
- [ ] AI-recommended purchase orders: ASIN, suggested quantity, estimated cost, ship-by date
- [ ] Editable quantities before approval
- [ ] One-click "Create Inbound Shipment" → pushes to Fulfillment Inbound API
- [ ] Calendar view of planned replenishments

## Epic: EP20 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [EPIC] EP21: Frontend — Pricing Management Module
body:
## Epic: Pricing Management Module

**Objective:** Design the pricing management interface for monitoring competitive prices, Buy Box status, and AI repricing decisions.

**Phase:** 1 (Month 3-4)

**Key Screens:**
- Pricing overview dashboard
- Product pricing detail with competitor comparison
- Buy Box tracker
- Pricing rules configuration
- Price history chart

title: [STORY] Pricing dashboard with Buy Box tracker and competitor map
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a pricing dashboard that shows my Buy Box win rate, competitor prices, and AI repricing activity,
**So that** I can monitor pricing strategy effectiveness and override AI decisions when needed.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Buy Box Summary
- [ ] Overall Buy Box win rate (%) with trend
- [ ] ASINs won / lost / not eligible breakdown
- [ ] Top 5 ASINs by Buy Box improvement (AI impact)
- [ ] Top 5 ASINs needing attention (lost Buy Box recently)

### Pricing Table
- [ ] Columns: image, ASIN, your price, Buy Box price, lowest competitor price, number of sellers, Buy Box winner (you/competitor), margin %, last repriced, AI status
- [ ] Visual indicator when your price ≠ Buy Box price
- [ ] Inline price edit with margin recalculation preview
- [ ] "Override" button to manually set price (overrides AI)
- [ ] Expand row: competitor price table (seller name, price, fulfillment, rating)

### Price History Chart (per ASIN)
- [ ] Your price, Buy Box price, and top 3 competitor prices over time
- [ ] AI reprice events annotated on the chart
- [ ] Sales velocity overlay to show price↔demand relationship

### Pricing Rules Configuration
- [ ] Minimum and maximum price per ASIN or category
- [ ] Target margin % (floor)
- [ ] Competitor response strategy: match, undercut by X%, maintain premium
- [ ] Schedule: different rules for weekday vs weekend, promotional periods
- [ ] "Pause repricing" toggle per ASIN or globally

## Epic: EP21 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [EPIC] EP22: Frontend — Analytics & Reporting Module
body:
## Epic: Analytics & Reporting Module

**Objective:** Design comprehensive analytics screens covering profitability, sales performance, and AI-generated business intelligence.

**Phase:** 2 (Months 7-8)

**Key Screens:**
- P&L dashboard
- Product performance matrix
- Sales analytics (by time, category, marketplace)
- Custom report builder
- Competitive intelligence dashboard

title: [STORY] P&L dashboard and product profitability matrix
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a visual P&L dashboard that shows true profitability per product, brand, and marketplace,
**So that** I can identify where I'm making money and where I'm losing it.

## Acceptance Criteria

### P&L Overview
- [ ] Revenue waterfall chart: gross revenue → refunds → Amazon fees → ad spend → COGS → net profit
- [ ] Period selector: daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, custom range
- [ ] Comparison toggle: vs previous period, vs same period last year
- [ ] Profit margin trend line chart

### Product Profitability Matrix
- [ ] Scatter plot: X-axis = revenue, Y-axis = margin %, bubble size = units sold
- [ ] Color-coded: green (profitable + growing), amber (profitable + flat), red (unprofitable)
- [ ] Click bubble → drill into product detail
- [ ] Quadrant labels: "Stars", "Cash cows", "Question marks", "Losers"

### Cost Breakdown Table
- [ ] Per-SKU: revenue, COGS, FBA fees, referral fee, ad spend, returns, shipping, net profit, margin %
- [ ] Sortable by any metric
- [ ] AI recommendation column: "Increase price by $2", "Cut ad spend", "Discontinue"
- [ ] Export to CSV/Excel

### AI Business Intelligence
- [ ] Weekly AI-generated business summary: top insights, risks, opportunities
- [ ] Trend alerts: "Product X margin dropped 8% this week due to increased ad spend"
- [ ] Actionable recommendations linked to agent actions

## Epic: EP22 | Priority: P1 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Competitor intelligence dashboard
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a competitor intelligence dashboard that visualizes market dynamics and competitive threats,
**So that** I can anticipate competitor moves and adjust my strategy proactively.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Competitor watchlist: tracked ASINs with price, rating, review count, BSR, listing changes
- [ ] Market share estimation chart (pie/bar) based on Brand Analytics data
- [ ] Search term performance: my click share vs competitor click share over time
- [ ] Price positioning map: scatter plot of price vs rating for category, with my products highlighted
- [ ] New entrant alerts: new ASINs detected in my categories
- [ ] Competitor listing change feed: timeline of detected changes (price drops, new images, title changes)
- [ ] AI competitive response recommendations: "Competitor X dropped price 15%, consider matching" with one-click approve

## Epic: EP22 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP23: Frontend — Settings, Team & Account Management
body:
## Epic: Settings, Team & Account Management

**Objective:** Design all settings, team management, and account configuration screens.

**Phase:** 1 (Month 3-4)

**Key Screens:**
- General settings
- Agent configuration
- Team member management
- Connected accounts (Amazon SP-API, Ads)
- Notification preferences
- API keys (for developer tier)

title: [STORY] Settings and team management screens
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** comprehensive settings pages to manage my account, team, connected services, and agent preferences,
**So that** I can customize the platform to my business needs and delegate access to my team.

## Acceptance Criteria

### General Settings
- [ ] Company name, logo upload (for white-label agency tier)
- [ ] Default marketplace selection
- [ ] Timezone and currency preferences
- [ ] Business mode toggle (growth vs profit)

### Connected Accounts
- [ ] Amazon SP-API connection status per marketplace (green/red indicator)
- [ ] Reconnect / disconnect buttons
- [ ] Permission summary: which API roles are authorized
- [ ] Amazon Ads API connection status
- [ ] MCP Server connection health indicator
- [ ] Last sync timestamp for each connection

### Agent Configuration
- [ ] Per-agent autonomy settings (visual slider: manual → supervised → autonomous)
- [ ] Per-agent toggle: enabled/disabled
- [ ] Global agent parameters: target ACoS, min margin, max daily spend
- [ ] Agent schedule: active hours, pause on weekends option
- [ ] Brand voice configuration for content generation

### Team Management
- [ ] Invite team members by email
- [ ] Roles: Owner, Admin, Manager, Viewer
- [ ] Per-role permission matrix (which modules they can access, can they approve actions)
- [ ] Activity log per team member
- [ ] Remove / deactivate team members

### Notification Preferences
- [ ] Per-notification-type toggle matrix: in-app, email, push
- [ ] Frequency options: instant, hourly digest, daily digest
- [ ] Critical alert override: always notify regardless of preferences

## Epic: EP23 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [EPIC] EP24: Frontend — Integration Layer & Real-Time Architecture
body:
## Epic: Integration Layer & Real-Time Architecture

**Objective:** Build the frontend integration layer: API client, WebSocket connection, state management, and data flow architecture.

**Phase:** 1 (Month 2-3)

**Key Components:**
- API client with auth and tenant context
- WebSocket connection manager for real-time updates
- Frontend state management (React Query + Zustand)
- Optimistic updates and error handling patterns
- Data caching and invalidation strategy

**Related Stories:** #75 through #81

title: [STORY] API client with authentication and tenant-scoped requests
body:
## User Story

**As a** frontend developer,
**I want** a typed API client that handles authentication, tenant context, and error states,
**So that** every API call is properly authenticated and scoped to the current tenant.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Axios or Fetch wrapper with automatic JWT injection from Cognito session
- [ ] Tenant ID injected into every request header or query parameter
- [ ] Automatic token refresh on 401 responses (silent re-auth)
- [ ] Request retry logic with exponential backoff for 5xx errors
- [ ] Global error boundary: toast notifications for API errors
- [ ] Request cancellation for unmounted components (AbortController)
- [ ] TypeScript types generated from backend OpenAPI spec
- [ ] Request/response interceptors for logging and analytics
- [ ] Rate limit handling: queue and retry on 429 responses
- [ ] Marketplace context header for multi-marketplace requests

## Technical Notes
- Use React Query for server state management and caching
- Generate TypeScript types from FastAPI's auto-generated OpenAPI schema
- Consider tRPC if backend supports it for end-to-end type safety

## Epic: EP24 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] WebSocket connection manager for real-time updates
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** the dashboard to update in real-time without refreshing the page,
**So that** I see agent actions, inventory changes, and new orders the instant they happen.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] WebSocket connection to backend (via API Gateway WebSocket or Socket.io)
- [ ] Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff on disconnection
- [ ] Connection status indicator in UI (connected/reconnecting/disconnected)
- [ ] Event types supported: agent_action, approval_request, metric_update, notification, inventory_alert, price_change, order_new
- [ ] React context provider for WebSocket: any component can subscribe to specific event types
- [ ] Optimistic UI updates: show changes immediately, roll back on server rejection
- [ ] Message queue for offline: buffer events during disconnection, replay on reconnect
- [ ] Tenant-scoped channels: only receive events for current tenant
- [ ] Heartbeat ping/pong to detect stale connections

## Technical Notes
- Use native WebSocket API with custom reconnection logic (avoid heavy libraries)
- React Query cache invalidation on WebSocket events (e.g., invalidate inventory query on inventory_alert)
- Consider Server-Sent Events (SSE) as simpler alternative for one-way feeds

## Epic: EP24 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Frontend state management architecture
body:
## User Story

**As a** frontend developer,
**I want** a clear state management architecture separating server state from client state,
**So that** data fetching, caching, and UI state are predictable and performant.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] React Query (TanStack Query) for all server state: API data fetching, caching, background refetch, pagination
- [ ] Zustand for client-only UI state: sidebar open/closed, selected filters, modal state, user preferences
- [ ] Query key strategy: `[entity, tenantId, marketplaceId, ...filters]` for proper cache scoping
- [ ] Stale-while-revalidate for dashboard data (show cached, refetch in background)
- [ ] Infinite scroll / cursor-based pagination for large tables (activity feed, orders)
- [ ] Prefetching: hover-to-prefetch on navigation links
- [ ] Persisted client state: filters, column preferences, widget layout saved to localStorage
- [ ] DevTools integration: React Query Devtools, Zustand middleware for debugging

## Epic: EP24 | Priority: P0 | Points: 5

title: [STORY] SP-API connector service integration
body:
## User Story

**As a** backend developer,
**I want** a reusable SP-API connector service that handles authentication, rate limiting, and data transformation for all Amazon API calls,
**So that** all agents and services share a consistent, reliable interface to Amazon.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Centralized SP-API client with Login with Amazon (LWA) token management
- [ ] Automatic token refresh before expiry (refresh tokens encrypted per-tenant)
- [ ] Rate limit tracking per endpoint per seller: in-memory counter + Redis backing
- [ ] Adaptive throttling: slow down requests before hitting rate limits, not after
- [ ] Request queue with priority levels: agent-initiated (normal), user-initiated (high), notification-handler (critical)
- [ ] Response caching layer: cache stable data (catalog info) for hours, volatile data (prices, inventory) for minutes
- [ ] Error classification: retryable (429, 503) vs non-retryable (400, 403) with appropriate handling
- [ ] API call logging: every call logged with tenant_id, endpoint, response time, status for debugging and billing
- [ ] Sandbox mode: route calls to SP-API sandbox environment for testing
- [ ] Supported API groups: Listings, Catalog, Pricing, Inventory, Orders, Feeds, Reports, Notifications, Finances, A+ Content, Data Kiosk

## Technical Notes
- Use python-amazon-sp-api library or custom implementation with requests
- Implement as a shared microservice consumed by all agents
- Rate limits vary by endpoint and seller — maintain per-seller-per-endpoint counters

## Epic: EP24 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Amazon Ads MCP Server integration service
body:
## User Story

**As a** backend developer,
**I want** a robust integration service for the Amazon Ads MCP Server,
**So that** the advertising agent can manage campaigns via natural-language MCP tools with proper error handling and fallback.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] MCP client connection to Amazon Ads MCP Server endpoint
- [ ] Support for all MCP tool prefixes: profile management, campaign CRUD (cp_*), ad group management, keyword management, targeting, reporting, budget management
- [ ] Per-tenant MCP session management with Ads API credentials
- [ ] Region auto-routing: NA, EU, FE based on seller's marketplace
- [ ] Async report handling: initiate report → poll for completion → retrieve results
- [ ] Graceful degradation: fallback to direct Amazon Ads API v3 if MCP Server is unavailable
- [ ] Response parsing and normalization: transform MCP responses into platform's internal data model
- [ ] MCP interaction logging: every prompt → response logged for debugging and audit
- [ ] Rate limit awareness: respect MCP Server rate limits separate from direct API limits
- [ ] Error handling for MCP-specific failures: deprecated API path detection, execution timeout, malformed response

## Technical Notes
- MCP Server acts as translation layer — converts natural language to API calls
- SP data lookback: 95 days, SB/SD lookback: 60 days
- MCP cannot see inventory or margin data — must inject from Inventory and Analytics agents
- During internal testing, Amazon found agents sometimes take inefficient paths — implement guardrails

## Epic: EP24 | Priority: P0 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Amazon SP-API notification webhook handler
body:
## User Story

**As a** platform,
**I want** a notification handler that receives and processes all Amazon SP-API notifications in real-time,
**So that** agents can react immediately to price changes, inventory events, listing issues, and order updates.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SQS queue subscriber for Amazon SP-API notifications (SQS destination)
- [ ] Supported notification types:
  - ANY_OFFER_CHANGED — competitor price changes → Pricing Agent
  - B2B_ANY_OFFER_CHANGED — B2B price changes → Pricing Agent
  - LISTINGS_ITEM_STATUS_CHANGE — listing activated/deactivated → Listing Agent
  - LISTINGS_ITEM_ISSUES_CHANGE — quality issues detected → Listing Agent + Compliance Agent
  - ITEM_INVENTORY_EVENT_CHANGE — stock level changes → Inventory Agent
  - ITEM_SALES_EVENT_CHANGE — hourly sales data → Analytics Agent
  - ORDER_CHANGE — order status updates → Order Agent
  - PRODUCT_TYPE_DEFINITIONS_CHANGE — catalog requirement changes → Listing Agent
- [ ] Tenant identification from notification payload → route to correct tenant's agent pipeline
- [ ] Idempotency: deduplicate notifications (Amazon may send duplicates)
- [ ] Dead letter queue for failed processing with alerting
- [ ] Notification processing latency: < 5 seconds from receipt to agent action
- [ ] Per-tenant notification subscription management (create/update/delete subscriptions)
- [ ] Health monitoring: alert if notification flow stops unexpectedly

## Epic: EP24 | Priority: P0 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Data Kiosk API integration for analytics and Brand Analytics
body:
## User Story

**As a** backend developer,
**I want** a Data Kiosk API integration that submits custom GraphQL queries and processes bulk analytics data,
**So that** the analytics and keyword agents have access to sales, traffic, and Brand Analytics data.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Submit GraphQL queries via Data Kiosk API for: Sales and Traffic (Analytics_SalesAndTraffic_2024_04_24), Brand Analytics search terms, Brand Analytics repeat purchase behavior
- [ ] Handle async query lifecycle: submit → poll processing status → download result documents
- [ ] Parse and transform bulk data responses into platform's analytics data model
- [ ] Schedule recurring queries: daily sales/traffic refresh, weekly Brand Analytics pull
- [ ] Result retention awareness: respect @resultRetention directives (shortest retention applies)
- [ ] Granularity support: ASIN-level, date-level, marketplace-level aggregation
- [ ] Store processed analytics in data warehouse (Redshift) for historical trending
- [ ] Error handling: query validation failures, processing timeouts, quota limits
- [ ] Per-tenant query management: each tenant's queries isolated and tracked

## Technical Notes
- Data Kiosk uses GraphQL query operations — build a query builder UI or predefined query templates
- Schema Explorer can be used to discover available fields and their retention periods
- Brand Analytics requires Brand Analytics SP-API role + Brand Registry enrollment

## Epic: EP24 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Stripe billing integration service
body:
## User Story

**As a** platform operator,
**I want** a complete Stripe integration for subscription billing, usage-based charges, and invoicing,
**So that** billing is automated and sellers can self-serve their subscription management.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Stripe Customer creation on tenant onboarding
- [ ] Subscription creation for selected plan tier
- [ ] Stripe Checkout Session for secure payment collection
- [ ] Webhook handler for Stripe events: payment_succeeded, payment_failed, subscription_updated, subscription_canceled, invoice.created
- [ ] Usage-based billing: report AI agent action counts via Stripe Usage Records
- [ ] Ad spend commission tracking: monthly metered billing for managed ad spend above tier limits
- [ ] Prorated upgrades/downgrades
- [ ] Dunning management: retry failed payments, grace period, eventual suspension
- [ ] Invoice PDF generation and email delivery
- [ ] Stripe Customer Portal integration for self-service billing management
- [ ] Revenue analytics: MRR, churn rate, ARPU tracked and displayed in admin panel

## Epic: EP24 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Frontend error handling, loading states, and empty states
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** clear feedback when data is loading, when errors occur, and when sections have no data yet,
**So that** I never stare at a blank screen wondering if something is broken.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Loading States
- [ ] Skeleton loaders matching the shape of content being loaded (not generic spinners)
- [ ] Table skeleton: animated row placeholders
- [ ] Chart skeleton: animated chart shape placeholder
- [ ] Card skeleton: animated metric card placeholder
- [ ] Progressive loading: show available data immediately, load remaining in background

### Error States
- [ ] Global error boundary: catches unhandled errors, shows friendly message + "Report issue" button
- [ ] API error toast: "Something went wrong. Retrying..." with manual retry button
- [ ] Amazon API specific errors: "Amazon connection temporarily unavailable" with auto-retry countdown
- [ ] Validation errors: inline field-level errors with clear fix instructions
- [ ] Offline indicator: banner when internet connection is lost

### Empty States (per module)
- [ ] Listings: "No listings yet — connect your Amazon account to get started" with illustration + CTA
- [ ] Advertising: "No campaigns running — let AI create your first campaign" + CTA
- [ ] Inventory: "Inventory data loading..." or "Connect FBA to see inventory"
- [ ] Analytics: "Not enough data yet — check back in 24 hours"
- [ ] Each empty state has a relevant illustration and a single clear call-to-action

### Transition States
- [ ] "Submitting to Amazon..." progress indicator for API submissions
- [ ] "AI is thinking..." animated indicator during agent processing
- [ ] "Waiting for Amazon approval..." status for moderation-pending content

## Epic: EP15 | Priority: P1 | Points: 5

title: [STORY] Responsive design and mobile optimization
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller on the go,
**I want** the dashboard to be fully usable on my tablet and readable on my phone,
**So that** I can approve urgent agent actions and check business health from anywhere.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Tablet (640-1024px)
- [ ] Sidebar collapses to icons-only by default
- [ ] Dashboard grid: 2 columns instead of 3
- [ ] Tables: horizontal scroll with frozen first column (product name)
- [ ] Charts: full-width, touch-friendly tooltips
- [ ] Modals: full-width with appropriate padding

### Mobile (< 640px)
- [ ] Bottom navigation bar replacing sidebar (5 key sections)
- [ ] Dashboard: single column, stacked widgets
- [ ] Priority mobile actions: approve/reject from notification, quick metrics view
- [ ] Tables: card view toggle (each row becomes a card)
- [ ] Touch targets: minimum 44px tap targets
- [ ] Pull-to-refresh on main dashboard
- [ ] Swipe gestures: swipe to approve/reject in approval queue

### Cross-device
- [ ] Responsive images: proper srcset for different screen densities
- [ ] Font sizes: minimum 16px for body text (prevent iOS zoom)
- [ ] No horizontal scrolling on any screen at any breakpoint
- [ ] Test: Chrome DevTools responsive mode for all screens

## Epic: EP15 | Priority: P2 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] A+ Content and Brand Store visual editor
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a visual drag-and-drop editor for A+ Content modules and Brand Store pages,
**So that** I can preview and customize AI-generated enhanced content before publishing.

## Acceptance Criteria

### A+ Content Editor
- [ ] Module palette: drag modules from sidebar onto content canvas
- [ ] Supported modules: comparison chart, standard text + image, brand story header, feature highlights, product description
- [ ] AI-generated content pre-populated in each module
- [ ] Inline text editing with rich formatting
- [ ] Image upload and crop tool per module
- [ ] Live preview: renders as it would appear on Amazon product page
- [ ] Mobile preview toggle
- [ ] Module reordering via drag-and-drop
- [ ] Submit for moderation with status tracking

### Brand Store Editor
- [ ] Page-level management: add, remove, reorder pages
- [ ] Section-based layout within pages
- [ ] Product grid management: drag products into store sections
- [ ] Header/hero section customization
- [ ] Navigation structure editor
- [ ] Section-level performance metrics overlay (renders, clicks, CTR from Stores Analytics API)
- [ ] Submit for moderation with status tracking

## Epic: EP18 | Priority: P1 | Points: 13

title: [STORY] Compliance and account health dashboard
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** a dedicated compliance dashboard showing my account health status, policy issues, and regulatory requirements,
**So that** I can prevent account suspensions and stay compliant across all marketplaces.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Account Health Score: composite score with color coding (green/amber/red)
- [ ] Metric cards: Order Defect Rate, Late Shipment Rate, Pre-Fulfillment Cancel Rate, each vs Amazon's threshold
- [ ] Policy violation log: list of current and resolved violations with status and resolution steps
- [ ] Listing restriction checker: search by ASIN to see if it can be listed in specific categories/marketplaces
- [ ] Product safety documentation tracker: which products need docs, which are compliant, which are overdue
- [ ] EU compliance panel: EPR registration status, VAT configuration per marketplace
- [ ] IP complaint tracker: received complaints, response status, resolution timeline
- [ ] AI recommendations: proactive steps to improve account health
- [ ] Historical trend charts for each health metric

## Epic: EP22 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Promotions and deals management interface
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** an interface to plan, create, and track promotional deals and coupons,
**So that** I can manage my promotional calendar and measure deal ROI in one place.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Promotional calendar: month view showing planned, active, and completed deals
- [ ] Deal creation wizard: type selection (Lightning Deal, Best Deal, Coupon, Prime Exclusive) → product selection → discount configuration → schedule → review → submit
- [ ] AI recommendation cards: "Recommended deals this month" based on inventory, seasonality, and margin
- [ ] Active deals dashboard: current deals with real-time performance (units sold, revenue, redemption rate)
- [ ] Deal history with ROI analysis: direct sales + organic rank impact + review velocity
- [ ] Coupon management: active coupons, redemption tracking, budget burn rate
- [ ] Budget tracker: promotional spend vs budget allocation
- [ ] Calendar sync: integration with inventory replenishment calendar to ensure stock for deals

## Epic: EP22 | Priority: P2 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Multi-marketplace switcher and unified cross-market view
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller operating in multiple Amazon marketplaces,
**I want** seamless switching between marketplaces and a unified view across all of them,
**So that** I can manage my global business from a single interface.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Marketplace selector in top bar with flag icons (US, CA, MX, UK, DE, FR, IT, ES, JP, AU, etc.)
- [ ] Instant context switch: all dashboard data, listings, campaigns refresh for selected marketplace
- [ ] "All Marketplaces" unified view option for cross-market metrics
- [ ] Unified P&L: aggregate revenue and costs across all marketplaces with currency conversion
- [ ] Cross-marketplace product mapping: same product shown across all marketplaces it's listed in
- [ ] Performance comparison: side-by-side metrics for same product across different marketplaces
- [ ] Marketplace health indicator: red/amber/green per marketplace in the selector
- [ ] Currency display: native marketplace currency with USD equivalent

## Epic: EP23 | Priority: P2 | Points: 8

title: [STORY] Agent decision audit trail and history viewer
body:
## User Story

**As a** seller,
**I want** to see the complete decision history for any ASIN — every action taken by every agent with reasoning,
**So that** I can understand why agents made specific decisions and build trust in the automation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Per-ASIN timeline view: chronological feed of all agent actions affecting this product
- [ ] Each entry shows: timestamp, agent name + icon, action taken, reasoning summary, outcome (success/failure), impact metrics
- [ ] Expandable detail: full agent prompt, context used, alternatives considered, confidence score
- [ ] Filter by agent type, action type, date range, outcome
- [ ] Cross-agent workflow view: show how agents coordinated (e.g., "Inventory Agent detected low stock → Advertising Agent reduced bids → Pricing Agent increased price")
- [ ] "Undo" capability for reversible actions (within time window)
- [ ] Export audit trail to CSV for compliance/reporting
- [ ] Search by action type or keyword within audit trail
- [ ] Admin view: aggregate audit across all tenants for platform debugging

## Epic: EP22 | Priority: P1 | Points: 8
